verified_against: Schedule TOU-DR1, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64819-E (Sheet 2, rates) and Sheet 64382-E (Sheet 5, time periods), Advice Ltr. 4843-E, effective Jun 1 2026, submitted May 29 2026

notes.delivery_composition: delivery price_per_kwh = UDC Total 0.32948 + (WF-NBC 0.00591 + DWR-BC 0.00000) = 0.33539. Per Sheet 2 note 5. These are non-bypassable and apply to bundled and unbundled customers alike, so they sit on the delivery side rather than in cca_adders. Note 3: they do not apply to CARE customers.
notes.flat_delivery: UDC Total is identical across every TOU period and both seasons FOR TOU-DR1 SPECIFICALLY. Do not generalize: a real EV-TOU-5 bill shows delivery of $0.31711 on-peak and off-peak but $0.04114 super-off-peak, so other schedules do vary delivery by period. The schema's per-period delivery blocks are needed.
notes.unmodeled_charges: A real bill also carries charges this schema has no home for: State Regulatory Fee (450 kWh x $0.001000), a 'Franchise Fee Equivalent Surcharge' (6.88%) applied to a different base than the City of San Diego Franchise Fee Differential (5.78%), and an Economic Development Program Credit. Together these were ~$9.72 of a $110.97 electric bill — roughly 9%, too large to ignore.
unverified: baseline.climate_zones is empty — allowances are published in a separate baseline schedule, not in this document.
day_types.holidays is empty — the observed-holiday list is not in this schedule. Known so far from bill validation: Juneteenth (Jun 19) is NOT observed. See rates/VALIDATION.md.

PCIA is vintaged by the year the customer left bundled service — 2009-2026 span 0.01538-0.05055, a 3x range. pcia_per_kwh defaults to the 2026 vintage; the calculator needs a vintage selector or most CCA comparisons will be wrong.
Whether CTC is owed on top of delivery by CCA customers is UNRESOLVED — see cca_adders._formula. This is the largest remaining correctness risk in this file.
fixed_charges.minimum_bill_daily omitted at file level — no minimum bill line appears on the TOU schedules. EV-TOU does have one and carries its own fixed_charges block.

schedules_not_modeled.care_fera_medical: CARE (E-CARE), FERA, and Medical Baseline are discounts applied to the schedules above, not standalone plans. Each TOU sheet prints a parallel -CARE/-LI/-MB rate table. Not modeled; the discounted daily service charges are already in fixed_charges.
schedules_not_modeled.multi_family: DM, DS, DT, DT-RV (submetered multi-family) and DE (utility employees) are out of scope for an individually metered residential calculator.
schedules_not_modeled.e_smop: E-SMOP is a smart-meter opt-out fee, not a rate schedule. A customer who opts out has no interval data to import in the first place.

baseline._source: Schedule DR, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64339-E (Sheet 4), Special Condition 3, Advice Ltr. 4791-E effective Apr 1 2026. Units are kWh per day.
baseline._all_electric: All-Electric allowances require permanently installed electric space heating, or electric water heating with no energy from another source, and must be applied for.
baseline._zone_lookup: Climatic zones are defined by SDG&E 'Territory Served, Map No. 1' (tarfKey 187) — a raster map of geographic bands labeled by city, with no zip codes on it. There is no published zip-to-zone list, so v1 has the user pick their zone directly; their bill prints it.

day_types._source: Electric Rule 1 (ERULE1, tarfKey 77), 'HOLIDAYS' definition, Advice Ltr. 4375-E effective Jul 1 2024. Every TOU schedule cross-references this rule rather than listing dates itself.
day_types._observance: When a listed holiday falls on a SUNDAY, the following Monday is the holiday instead. When it falls on a Saturday, no change is made — there is no Friday observance.
day_types._juneteenth: Juneteenth (Jun 19) is NOT a TOU holiday. Rule 1 omits it, which independently confirms the earlier bill-validation finding — adding it made the known-answer test worse. See rates/VALIDATION.md.
day_types._weekday_numbering: weekday follows ISO: 1=Monday through 7=Sunday. nth counts occurrences in the month; -1 means the last.
day_types._why_rules_not_dates: Stored as rules rather than a date list so the calculator works for any year of imported data without a rate-file update. A hardcoded list would silently go stale and mis-price holidays in older or newer exports.

taxes_and_fees._note: Applied after Total Electric Charges. On the validated bill these came to $9.72 of $110.97 (~9%). All three bases were derived arithmetically from one bill and match to the cent — see rates/VALIDATION.md.
taxes_and_fees.state_regulatory_fee_source: Schedule E-PUC, 'Surcharge to Fund Public Utilities Commission Reimbursement Fee', $0.001 per kWh for all service, Advice Ltr. 4763-E effective Jan 1 2026. Applies to all tariff rate schedules.
taxes_and_fees.franchise_fee_differential_applies_to: customers within the corporate limits of the City of San Diego
taxes_and_fees.franchise_fee_equivalent_surcharge_appli: CCA customers only — the utility imputes its own Schedule EECC and charges franchise fees on that commodity amount (Schedule CCA, per D.97-10-087). Entities of the State of California or a political subdivision are exempt.
taxes_and_fees.franchise_fee_equivalent_surcharge_unver: The 6.88% rate is NOT published in Schedule CCA or CCA-CRS — it appears to be city-set. Taken from the bill. The base derivation (imputed EECC + CARE surcharge at 0.00437/kWh) reproduces the bill's 65.44 exactly, but is inferred from a single bill, not stated in any tariff.

cca_adders._formula: Applicable CCA-CRS = DWR-BC + CTC + Applicable PCIA + WF-NBC (Schedule CCA-CRS). RESOLVED against a real SDCP customer bill (Jun 2026): CTC is NOT a separate charge — it appears only in the 'your charges include the following' disclosure box, not as a line item, so it is already inside the delivery rate. Add PCIA only. WF-NBC does bill as its own line (450 kWh x $0.00591) rather than inside delivery, but since it is flat per-kWh the folded delivery price here is arithmetically identical.
cca_adders.pcia_source: Schedule CCA-CRS, Advice Ltr. 4791-E-A, effective Apr 1 2026 — Residential customer class row
cca_adders.franchise_fee_applies_to: customers within the corporate limits of the City of San Diego only

plans.name: DR — Residential Service (tiered, non-TOU)
plans._source: Schedule DR, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64799-E (Sheet 1, rates), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.not_time_of_use: DR prices by cumulative consumption against the baseline allowance, not by clock hour. Time of day is irrelevant to the bill. This is SDG&E's default residential schedule.
_notes.tier_boundary: The boundary is 130% of the baseline allowance for the customer's climate zone, accumulated over the billing period: allowance_kwh_per_day x days_in_period x 1.30.
_notes.prices_identical_by_season: Summer and winter tier prices are identical on this revision (UDC 0.22285 / 0.32948, EECC 0.18274 flat). Both seasons are still written out, because the sheet publishes them as separate rows and a future revision can diverge them.
_notes.baseline_credit_is_folded_in: DR has no separate Baseline Adjustment Credit row. The tier-1 UDC breakdown on Sheet 3 shows a (0.04043) credit component already inside the 0.22285 — do NOT also apply baseline.credit_per_kwh to this plan. That is why 'dr' is absent from baseline.credit_applies_to_plans.
_notes.generation_is_flat: EECC is 0.18274 in both tiers and both seasons, so only delivery is actually tiered. The generation tiers are written out anyway to keep one shape per pricing model.
_notes.climate_credit: Semi-annual California Climate Credit of $49.36 per Schedule GHG-ARR. Not modeled.

eligibility.notes: SDG&E's default residential schedule; no interval meter required. Recorded, not enforced yet.
SDG&E standard residential schedule. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: EV-TOU-5 — Cost-Based Domestic Time-of-Use for Households with Electric Vehicles
plans._source: Schedule EV-TOU-5, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64817-E (Sheet 1-2), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.delivery: UDC 0.31711 on/off-peak and 0.04114 super-off-peak, each + WF-NBC 0.00591. Unlike TOU-DR1, delivery on this schedule DOES vary by TOU period — super-off-peak delivery is ~7x cheaper. Both delivery figures were confirmed exactly against a real bill (see rates/VALIDATION.md).
_notes.no_baseline_credit: This schedule has no Baseline Adjustment Credit row, unlike TOU-DR1. A real EV-TOU-5 bill shows no baseline credit line, which is consistent.
_notes.time_periods: Identical windows to TOU-DR1 — verified from both tariff sheets, not assumed.
_notes.climate_credit: Customers may also receive a semi-annual California Climate Credit of $49.36 per Schedule GHG-ARR. Not modeled.

eligibility.notes: Requires an electric vehicle. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: TOU-DR — Residential Time of Use Service
plans._source: Schedule TOU-DR, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64801-E (Sheet 1, rates) and Sheet 5 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.delivery: UDC Total 0.32948 flat across all periods + WF-NBC 0.00591 = 0.33539. Same delivery as TOU-DR1.
_notes.vs_tou_dr1: Same delivery and same TOU windows as TOU-DR1; only the EECC generation prices differ. TOU-DR has a much flatter generation curve — summer on-peak 0.22895 vs TOU-DR1's 0.34920.
_notes.baseline_credit: Has the Baseline Adjustment Credit, both seasons.

eligibility.notes: Optional residential schedule; requires interval metering. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: TOU-DR2 — Residential Time-of-Use (two-period)
plans._source: Schedule TOU-DR2, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64822-E (Sheet 2, rates) and Sheet 5 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4791-E effective Apr 1 2026

_notes.two_periods_only: TOU-DR2 has NO super-off-peak and no weekday/weekend distinction — the sheet labels its table 'Year Round' with on-peak 4-9pm and off-peak 9pm-4pm, every day. The weekday and weekend trees here are deliberately identical.
_notes.delivery_varies: Summer delivery differs by period: on-peak UDC 0.33396, off-peak UDC 0.32750. Winter is flat at 0.32948. All + WF-NBC 0.00591.
_notes.older_revision: This schedule was last revised by Advice Ltr. 4791-E (Apr 1 2026), not 4843-E (Jun 1 2026) like most of this file. That is correct, not stale — 4843-E did not touch TOU-DR2.
_notes.baseline_credit: Has the Baseline Adjustment Credit, both seasons.
_notes.not_applicable_to: Not available to multi-family schedules DM, DT, DS, DT-RV.

eligibility.notes: Available since Jan 1 2019 to domestic service with interval metering. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: TOU-ELEC — Domestic TOU for Households with EVs, Energy Storage, or Heat Pumps
plans._source: Schedule TOU-ELEC, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64825-E (Sheet 1, rates) and Sheet 4 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.delivery: UDC Total 0.25317 flat across all periods + WF-NBC 0.00591 = 0.25908. Lowest delivery of any residential schedule in this file — the plan recovers more through a steep on-peak generation price instead.
_notes.no_baseline_credit: No Baseline Adjustment Credit row on the sheet.
_notes.enrollment_cap: Capped at 10,000 customers per Special Condition 10. The calculator may rank this plan best for a user who cannot actually enroll — worth surfacing once eligibility is enforced.
_notes.climate_credit: Semi-annual California Climate Credit of $49.36 per Schedule GHG-ARR. Not modeled.

eligibility.notes: Requires an EV/NGV, a Rule 21 behind-the-meter storage device, or an electric heat pump. Capped at 10,000 customers. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: EV-TOU-2 — Domestic Time-of-Use for Households with Electric Vehicles
plans._source: Schedule EV-TOU-2, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64814-E (Sheet 1, rates) and Sheet 3 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.delivery: UDC 0.30372 on/off-peak and 0.16275 super-off-peak, each + WF-NBC 0.00591. Delivery varies by period, like EV-TOU-5.
_notes.shared_generation: EECC is identical to EV-TOU-5 and DR-SES and EV-TOU (0.47019 / 0.17311 / 0.08147 summer, 0.19990 / 0.14337 / 0.07410 winter). The four schedules differ only in delivery. This is a real feature of the tariff, not a copy-paste error.
_notes.no_baseline_credit: No Baseline Adjustment Credit row on the sheet.

eligibility.notes: Requires a registered BEV, PHEV, or NGV with home refueling. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: DR-SES — Domestic Time-of-Use for Households with a Solar Energy System
plans._source: Schedule DR-SES, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64804-E (Sheet 1, rates) and Sheet 3 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4791-E effective Apr 1 2026

_notes.delivery: UDC Total 0.26328 flat across all periods + WF-NBC 0.00591 = 0.26919.
_notes.shared_generation: Same EECC as EV-TOU-5 / EV-TOU-2 / EV-TOU.
_notes.solar_only: This schedule is for customers with a solar energy system, so its cost is only meaningful once NEM/export pricing is modeled — which is deferred. Costing it against import-only interval data will overstate the bill. Surface that caveat before ranking it.
_notes.no_baseline_credit: No Baseline Adjustment Credit row on the sheet.

eligibility.notes: Requires an individually metered solar energy system. Recorded, not enforced yet.

plans.name: EV-TOU — Domestic Time-of-Use for Electric Vehicle Charging (separately metered)
plans._source: Schedule EV-TOU, Cal. P.U.C. Sheet 64812-E (Sheet 1, rates) and Sheet 3 (time periods), Advice Ltr. 4843-E effective Jun 1 2026

_notes.separate_meter: EV-TOU requires the EV charging load to be SEPARATELY METERED from the house (Special Condition 2). It is not a whole-home schedule, so ranking it against a whole-home Green Button export is meaningless — the export covers the house meter, not the EV meter. Exclude it from the default comparison, or label it clearly.
_notes.delivery: UDC 0.37721 on/off-peak and 0.22056 super-off-peak, each + WF-NBC 0.00591.
_notes.fixed_charges: No Base Services Charge on this schedule — it has a minimum bill of $0.413/day instead. The file-level fixed_charges block does NOT apply here; see this plan's own fixed_charges.
_notes.shared_generation: Same EECC as EV-TOU-5 / EV-TOU-2 / DR-SES.

eligibility.notes: Separately metered EV or NGV charging load. Recorded, not enforced yet.
